cwe: CWE-22 - Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory (Path Traversal)
CWE-434 - Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
CWE-73 - External Control of File Name or Path

threat.title: File Upload Path Traversal in Express.js and Flask Applications
threat.description: Improper validation of file upload paths allows attackers to use directory traversal sequences (../, ..\ ) in filenames to write files outside intended upload directories. This can lead to remote code execution by overwriting critical system files, web application files, or uploading malicious executables to auto-execution directories. Common in applications using multer (Express.js) or custom file handling without proper sanitization.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies file upload endpoint in web application (e.g., /api/upload, /profile/avatar)
2. Intercepts upload request using Burp Suite or curl to modify filename parameter
3. Crafts malicious filename with path traversal sequences: '../../../etc/cron.d/evil' or '..\..\..\windows\system32\shell.php'
4. If using Express.js/multer without filename sanitization, uploads file with traversal path
5. Server writes file to unintended location due to unsanitized path concatenation
6. Attacker executes uploaded malicious file (web shell, cron job, startup script) to gain remote code execution
7. Escalates privileges and exfiltrates sensitive data or establishes persistent backdoor
prerequisites: Application accepts file uploads without proper filename sanitization
Multer configured with custom filename function that doesn't validate paths
Flask application using request.files without Werkzeug's secure_filename()
Server process has write permissions to critical directories
No Web Application Firewall (WAF) blocking path traversal patterns

cve_references.description: Apache Struts 2 file upload path traversal allowing RCE via manipulated file parameters. Affects versions 2.0.0-2.3.37, 2.5.0-2.5.32, 6.0.0-6.3.0. Attackers can upload malicious JSP files to arbitrary directories.

AnythingLLM path traversal via multer library's improper handling of non-ASCII filenames. Affects versions prior to 1.3.1. Allows authenticated users to write files to arbitrary locations leading to RCE.
PaperCut NG/MF path traversal in file upload enabling unauthenticated attackers to read, delete, and upload arbitrary files leading to RCE. Affects Windows versions prior to 22.1.3.

real_world_breaches.impact: Over 2,700 organizations affected including BBC, British Airways, and Zellis. Estimated $12 billion in damages. While primary vector was SQL injection, attackers used path traversal to access sensitive files.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Combination of SQL injection and path traversal in file transfer software allowing unauthorized file access and data exfiltration

real_world_breaches.impact: Path traversal incidents in closed-source projects surged from 1.9% in 2023 to 3.5% in 2024 (85% increase). Open-source saw increase from 2.6% to 2.75%.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Widespread improper file upload validation across web applications, particularly in Node.js/Express.js and Python/Flask frameworks

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Path traversal in profile picture upload allowing arbitrary file write and RCE via uploaded web shell

security_research.title: (In)Security of File Uploads in Node.js
security_research.key_finding: Comprehensive analysis of file upload vulnerabilities in Node.js, highlighting multer path traversal as critical security gap

security_research.title: Weak Multer File Name Manipulation
security_research.key_finding: Path traversal attacks occur when crafted filenames contain '..' characters that multer interprets as directory traversal, saving files to unintended locations

mitigations.title: Express.js: Secure Multer Configuration with Path Validation
mitigations.description: Implement strict filename sanitization in multer using UUID-based naming and path validation. Never trust user-supplied filenames. Store files outside webroot with restricted permissions.

tools.name: file-type
tools.cost: Free (npm package for magic number validation)

nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Test upload with filename containing '../../../etc/passwd' - should reject or sanitize
Verify uploaded files are stored with UUID names, not original filenames
Confirm upload directory is outside webroot and not directly accessible
Test file extension validation with .php, .jsp, .exe files - should reject
Check file permissions are restrictive (700 for directory, 600 for files)
Attempt to upload files larger than 5MB - should reject

mitigations.title: Flask: Werkzeug secure_filename() with Storage Isolation
mitigations.description: Use Werkzeug's secure_filename() to sanitize user-supplied filenames, combined with UUID prefixing and secure storage configuration. Implement type validation using python-magic for true MIME type detection.

code_example.code:
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify, send_from_directory
from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename
from werkzeug.datastructures import FileStorage
import os
import uuid
import magic  # python-magic for true MIME type detection
from pathlib import Path

app = Flask(__name__)

# Secure configuration
BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
UPLOAD_FOLDER = BASE_DIR / 'secure_uploads'  # Outside webroot
MAX_FILE_SIZE = 5 * 1024 * 1024  # 5MB
ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS = {'jpg', 'jpeg', 'png', 'gif', 'pdf'}
ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES = {
    'image/jpeg',
    'image/png',
    'image/gif',
    'application/pdf'
}

app.config['UPLOAD_FOLDER'] = str(UPLOAD_FOLDER)
app.config['MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH'] = MAX_FILE_SIZE

# Create upload directory with restricted permissions
UPLOAD_FOLDER.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True, mode=0o700)

def allowed_file(filename: str) -> bool:
    """Validate file extension"""
    return '.' in filename and \
           filename.rsplit('.', 1)[1].lower() in ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS

def validate_mime_type(file: FileStorage) -> bool:
    """Validate MIME type using magic numbers (file content inspection)"""
    # Read first 2048 bytes for magic number detection
    file_header = file.read(2048)
    file.seek(0)  # Reset file pointer
    
    mime = magic.from_buffer(file_header, mime=True)
    return mime in ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES

def generate_safe_filename(original_filename: str) -> str:
    """Generate cryptographically safe filename"""
    # Extract extension
    ext = Path(original_filename).suffix.lower()
    
    # Validate extension
    if ext not in [f'.{e}' for e in ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS]:
        raise ValueError(f'Invalid file extension: {ext}')
    
    # Generate unique filename
    unique_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
    safe_filename = f"{unique_id}{ext}"
    
    return safe_filename

def validate_upload_path(filepath: Path) -> bool:
    """Ensure resolved path is within upload directory (prevents path traversal)"""
    try:
        # Resolve to absolute path and check it's within UPLOAD_FOLDER
        resolved_path = filepath.resolve()
        upload_folder_resolved = UPLOAD_FOLDER.resolve()
        
        # CRITICAL: Check if resolved path starts with upload folder
        return str(resolved_path).startswith(str(upload_folder_resolved))
    except (ValueError, OSError):
        return False

@app.route('/api/upload', methods=['POST'])
def upload_file():
    # Check if file is present
    if 'file' not in request.files:
        return jsonify({'error': 'No file part in request'}), 400
    
    file = request.files['file']
    
    if file.filename == '':
        return jsonify({'error': 'No file selected'}), 400
    
    # Validate file extension
    if not allowed_file(file.filename):
        return jsonify({
            'error': f'Invalid file type. Allowed: {", ".join(ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS)}'
        }), 400
    
    # Validate MIME type via magic numbers
    if not validate_mime_type(file):
        return jsonify({'error': 'File content does not match allowed types'}), 400
    
    try:
        # SECURE: Generate safe filename (discard user input)
        safe_filename = generate_safe_filename(file.filename)
        
        # Construct full path
        file_path = UPLOAD_FOLDER / safe_filename
        
        # CRITICAL: Validate path doesn't escape upload directory
        if not validate_upload_path(file_path):
            app.logger.warning(f'Path traversal attempt detected: {file.filename}')
            return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid file path'}), 400
        
        # Save file with restricted permissions
        file.save(str(file_path))
        os.chmod(str(file_path), 0o600)  # Owner read/write only
        
        # Log upload for audit
        app.logger.info(f'File uploaded: {safe_filename}, Original: {file.filename}, Size: {file_path.stat().st_size}')
        
        return jsonify({
            'success': True,
            'fileId': safe_filename,
            'url': f'/api/files/{safe_filename}'
        }), 201
        
    except ValueError as e:
        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
    except Exception as e:
        app.logger.error(f'Upload error: {str(e)}')
        return jsonify({'error': 'Upload failed'}), 500

@app.route('/api/files/<filename>')
def download_file(filename):
    """Serve files securely using send_from_directory"""
    # SECURE: send_from_directory prevents path traversal
    # Additional validation
    file_path = UPLOAD_FOLDER / filename
    
    if not validate_upload_path(file_path) or not file_path.exists():
        return jsonify({'error': 'File not found'}), 404
    
    return send_from_directory(app.config['UPLOAD_FOLDER'], filename)

@app.errorhandler(413)
def file_too_large(e):
    return jsonify({'error': 'File too large (max 5MB)'}), 413

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=False)  # Never run debug mode in production

nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Install dependencies: pip install Flask python-magic
Test upload with '../../../etc/passwd' filename - should sanitize
Verify secure_filename() removes path traversal sequences
Test MIME type spoofing (rename .exe to .jpg) - should reject via magic numbers
Confirm files saved outside Flask static directory
Check file permissions are 600 (owner read/write only)

mitigations.title: Defense-in-Depth: Cloud Storage with Pre-Signed URLs
mitigations.description: Eliminate server-side file storage entirely by using cloud object storage (AWS S3, Azure Blob Storage) with pre-signed upload URLs. This prevents path traversal as files never touch application server filesystem.
validation_steps: Configure S3 bucket with block public access enabled
Create IAM role with PutObject and GetObject permissions only
Test upload flow: request pre-signed URL, upload to S3, verify file exists
Attempt to upload to different S3 key than provided - should fail
Verify Content-Type and size limits enforced by S3
Enable S3 server-side encryption (AES-256 or KMS)
Configure S3 lifecycle policies for automatic deletion of old files

indicators: File upload requests with filenames containing '../' or '..\\'
Files appearing in directories outside configured upload path
Unusual file extensions in upload directory (.php, .jsp, .exe, .sh)
Upload requests with null bytes (%00) in filenames
Repeated 400/500 errors during file upload (attack attempts)
Files with web shell patterns in upload directories
Unauthorized file modifications in system directories

log_queries.query:
// Detect path traversal patterns in file upload requests
W3CIISLog
| where csUriStem contains "/upload" or csUriStem contains "/files"
| where csUriQuery contains "../" or csUriQuery contains "..\\"
      or csUriStem contains "../" or csUriStem contains "..\\"
| extend Severity = "High"
| project TimeGenerated, cIP, csUriStem, csUriQuery, scStatus, csUserAgent
| order by TimeGenerated desc

index=web_logs sourcetype=access_combined
| where (uri_path="*upload*" OR uri_path="*files*")
  AND (uri_query="*../*" OR uri_query="*..\\*" OR form_data="*../*")
| stats count by clientip, uri_path, status, user_agent
| where count > 3
| eval severity="high"

// Detect suspicious S3 PutObject attempts
eventName="PutObject" AND (
  requestParameters.key="*../*" OR
  requestParameters.key="*/etc/*" OR
  requestParameters.key="*/var/*" OR
  requestParameters.key="*.php" OR
  requestParameters.key="*.jsp"
)

tools.name: OWASP ModSecurity CRS
tools.usage: Rule 930100 detects path traversal patterns in requests, blocks before reaching application

tools.name: Falco
tools.usage: Detects file writes outside expected directories: 'File opened for writing by app outside upload directory'

tools.name: OSSEC/Wazuh
tools.usage: Monitors system directories for unexpected file creation via inotify/FIM

compliance: SOC2 (CC6.6 - Logical and Physical Access Controls)
